title: pilosella collina subsp. elegans
intro: Pilosella collina subsp. elegans, also known as Alpine Mouse Ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The health benefits of Pilosella collina subsp. elegans are also attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and experience its health benefits.
